rationale: The citing paper discusses Kuksov and Liao (2018) as a key prior showrooming study and explicitly states its core result (showrooming can raise retailer profit under endogenous manufacturer-retailer contracts). It then uses that prior result to position its own contribution: moving beyond that stream to analyze assortment competition and return frictions in online-offline rivalry. This is more than a passing list citation because the cited paper is used to frame the conceptual gap and motivate the new model scope.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the showrooming literature represented by Kuksov and Liao (2018) and extends the setting to strategic assortment choice and product-return dynamics (including BORS) in online-offline competition. It preserves the showrooming context but shifts the mechanism from contracting-focused analysis to assortment and returns.
